source.title: Accident Investigation Report - VK 915 Platform A Gas Release

context.region: Gulf of America, Viosca Knoll Block 915

actions_taken: Control Room Operator traveled to Compressor #1 to investigate gas alarm
CRO called control room after witnessing gas release
Emergency Shutdown System (ESD) shut in compressor #1 and platform
Platform muster performed to account for all personnel
Compressor and scrubber isolated and pressure bled down
Broken pipe nipple removed and replaced
Facility returned to production after 30 minutes
event.summary: On November 24, 2024, at approximately 1710 hours, a 3/4 inch pipe nipple on the sight glass level bridle for Compressor #1 Suction Scrubber cracked and broke, causing a gas release at VK-915A (Marlin) Tension Leg Platform. The gas alarm activated, the Control Room Operator investigated and confirmed the release, and the Emergency Shutdown System shut in the platform. A platform muster was conducted with no injuries. The compressor and scrubber were isolated, the broken nipple was replaced within 30 minutes, and production resumed. Investigation determined the nipple failed due to fatigue cracking from multiple contributing factors including possible over-tightening during installation, vibration from the compressor, and excessive weight from the sight glass level bridle. The operator will submit a Management of Change to install additional support to prevent recurrence.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends that the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Anadarko to submit Management of Change (MOC) to install additional support to the Compressor #1 Suction Scrubber sight glass level bridle to reduce weight and vibration on the nipple

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in compression system

threats.name: Fatigue cracking of pipe nipple due to vibration
Excessive weight on pipe nipple from sight glass level bridle

consequences.name: Fire or explosion from gas contacting hot surfaces

controls.name: Gas detection system
controls.lod_basis: First automated system to detect the hazardous condition

supporting_text: the VK-915A platform control room received a gas alarm on the Compressor #1
The gas detectors detected the gas release and alarmed personnel

controls.name: Control Room Operator response to alarm
controls.barrier_role: Investigation and verification of alarm condition
controls.lod_basis: Human verification and response following automated detection

supporting_text: An Occidental Petroleum Control Room Operator (CRO) immediately traveled to the Compressor #1 and witnessed the nipple on the sight glass level bridle for the number one suction scrubber releasing gas
After seeing the gas release, the CRO called the control room

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown System (ESD)
controls.barrier_role: Automatic isolation and shutdown to stop gas release
controls.lod_basis: Automated safety system that isolates the hazard

supporting_text: The compressor #1 and the platform were then shut in by the Emergency Shutdown System (ESD)
The facility safety systems functioned as designed

controls.name: Platform muster procedure
controls.barrier_role: Personnel accountability and safety during emergency
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response procedure to protect personnel

supporting_text: Due to the gas alarm sounding, there was a platform muster performed to account for all personnel

controls.name: Isolation and pressure bleed down procedure
controls.barrier_role: Safe depressurization and isolation for repair
controls.lod_basis: Recovery action to make equipment safe for repair

supporting_text: The compressor and number one scrubber were then isolated, and the pressure was bled down

controls.name: Mechanical integrity inspection program
controls.barrier_role: Detection of equipment degradation before failure

supporting_text: it was determined that the 3/4 inch nipple had failed due to fatigue cracking
the nipple had suffered fatigue cracking which caused it to break
the nipple was originally installed over four years ago

controls.name: Proper installation procedures and torque specifications
controls.lod_basis: Work procedure to ensure correct installation

supporting_text: the over-tightening of the nipple when it was originally installed over four years ago

controls.name: Equipment design with adequate structural support
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of excessive weight and vibration on components
controls.lod_basis: Design control to prevent stress on components

supporting_text: Flawed equipment design – The insufficient support for the sight glass level bridle causing excessive weight on 3/4 inch nipple
the possibility of too much weight on the nipple from supporting the sight glass level bridle

Anadarko will be submitting a Management of Change (MOC) to install additional support to the Compressor #1 Suction Scrubber sight glass level bridle that will lower the amount of weight and vibration placed on the nipple

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
